name: Return to Monte San Marugan (PRC-VA 61) — circular from Megeces
name_original: Vuelta al Monte San Marugan (PRC-VA 61) — circular desde Megeces
region_intro: Hiking around Megeces offers a network of trails primarily characterized by gentle terrain and riverine landscapes. The region features a mix of open countryside, pine forests, and paths alongside waterways like the Eresma River. With mostly moderate difficulty routes, it provides accessible options for those seeking circular walks around Megeces. Best hiking trails around Megeces The most popular hiking route is Pino Pinilla Trail, an 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 42 minutes to complete. This route leads through local pine forests and open fields. Another top favourite among local hikers is Senda de la Resina (PRC-VA 38) — circular desde Puente Blanca, a moderate 4.9 miles (7.9 km) path. This circular route explores the local countryside and offers views of the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the Senda del Ermitaño and Presa de Alcazarén Loop, a 10.6 miles (17.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including the area around the Alcazarén reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 24 minutes. Hiking around Megeces is defined by river paths, open countryside, and pine woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on moderate routes. The routes in Megeces are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Megeces's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Megeces?
A: Megeces offers a network of over 10 hiking routes, primarily characterized by gentle terrain and riverine landscapes. Most of these trails are of moderate difficulty, making them accessible for a wide range of hikers.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Megeces?
A: Yes, Megeces is well-suited for circular walks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1083817589/senda-de-la-resina-prc-va-38-circular-desde-puente-blanca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de la Resina (PRC-VA 38) — circular desde Puente Blanca&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 4.9-mile (7.9 km) route that explores the local countryside. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079271497/on-the-banks-of-the-eresma-river-circular-from-alcazaren" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On the banks of the Eresma River — circular from Alcazarén&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Eresma River for about 7.8 miles (12.5 km).

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails around Megeces?
A: The trails around Megeces feature a mix of open countryside, local pine forests, and paths alongside waterways like the Eresma River. The terrain is generally gentle, with mostly moderate difficulty routes.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Megeces suitable for families?
A: Given that most routes are of moderate difficulty and feature gentle terrain, many trails around Megeces are suitable for families. The varied landscapes of pine forests and open fields offer pleasant environments for walks with children.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see near the trails?
A: While hiking around Megeces, you can explore several interesting landmarks. Consider visiting the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5780020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Church in Mojados&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5597863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Megeces Bridge&lt;/a&gt; itself is also a notable local feature.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Megeces?
A: The hiking routes in Megeces are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 6 reviews. Hikers often praise the accessible nature of the trails, the pleasant mix of pine forests and open countryside, and the tranquil river paths.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: While most trails are moderate, there is at least one route classified as difficult. For a longer moderate option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103826524/senda-del-ermitano-and-presa-de-alcazaren-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Ermitaño and Presa de Alcazarén Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers 10.6 miles (17.0 km) and leads through varied landscapes, including the area around the Alcazarén reservoir.

Q: Is Megeces a good destination for winter hiking?
A: Megeces generally experiences mild winters, which can make it suitable for hiking. However, conditions can vary, so it's always advisable to check local weather forecasts and trail conditions before heading out, especially for potential mud or colder temperatures.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Megeces?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in rural areas like Megeces, provided they are kept under control, especially around livestock or wildlife. Always ensure to clean up after your pet to maintain the natural environment for everyone.

Q: Where can I find parking when hiking in Megeces?
A: Specific parking information for individual trailheads is not always available in detail. However, in smaller towns and villages like Megeces and its surroundings, you can often find public parking areas near the start of popular routes or in the village centers.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of reservoirs or water bodies?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103826524/senda-del-ermitano-and-presa-de-alcazaren-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Ermitaño and Presa de Alcazarén Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically takes you through the area around the Alcazarén reservoir, offering scenic views of the water.

Q: What is the typical duration for a hike in Megeces?
A: Hiking durations in Megeces vary depending on the route. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079252978/pino-pinilla-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pino Pinilla Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.7-mile (14.0 km) route, typically takes around 3 hours 42 minutes, while shorter circular paths can be completed in less time.
tips: Viewpoint to the plain and agricultural fields around Megeces and to the forests of Mount Saint Marugan.
